Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class ConnectedApp

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.ConnectedApp
public class ConnectedApp extends Metadata

Java class for ConnectedApp complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="ConnectedApp">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="attributes" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppAttribute" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="canvas" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}CanvasMetadata" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="canvasConfig" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppCanvasConfig" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="contactEmail" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="contactPhone" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="description" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="iconUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="infoUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ipRanges" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppIpRange" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="label" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="logoUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="mobileAppConfig" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppMobileDetailConfig" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="mobileStartUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="oauthConfig" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppOauthConfig" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="oauthPolicy" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppOauthPolicy" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="permissionSetName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="plugin" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="pluginExecutionUser" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="profileName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="samlConfig" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppSamlConfig" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sessionPolicy" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ConnectedAppSessionPolicy" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="startUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getAttributes

      public List<ConnectedAppAttribute> getAttributes()
      Gets the value of the attributes property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the attributes property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getAttributes().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ConnectedAppAttribute

      Returns:
      The value of the attributes property.

    • getCanvas

      public CanvasMetadata getCanvas()
      Gets the value of the canvas property.
      Returns:
      possible object is CanvasMetadata

    • setCanvas

      Sets the value of the canvas property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is CanvasMetadata

      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is ConnectedAppCanvasConfig

    • getIpRanges

      public List<ConnectedAppIpRange> getIpRanges()
      Gets the value of the ipRanges property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the ipRanges property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getIpRanges().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ConnectedAppIpRange

      Returns:
      The value of the ipRanges property.

    • getPermissionSetName

      public List<String> getPermissionSetName()
      Gets the value of the permissionSetName property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the permissionSetName property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getPermissionSetName().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the permissionSetName property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the profileName property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getProfileName().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      The value of the profileName property.
